title: digitaria adscendens subsp. marginata
intro: Digitaria adscendens subsp. marginata, commonly known as finger grass, is a type of grass found in tropical regions, characterized by its finger-shaped leaves and ability to grow in a variety of environments. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with finger grass include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Finger grass can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
